verdict.headline: DO NOT TRANSACT — wallet is OFAC-sanctioned and linked to the North Korean Lazarus Group (Ronin Bridge hack).
verdict.reasoning: The sanctions screen returned a definitive hit against OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, and DPRK programs — this is a hard veto and overrides all other signals. Web sentiment independently corroborates the match, with multiple posts attributing the address to the 2022 Ronin Bridge $625M exploit and Lazarus Group activity. On-chain history shows ongoing token movement, indicating the wallet is still active, which only heightens the risk. Sending funds to this address would likely violate U.S. sanctions law and result in immediate loss of funds.

findings.finding: Address matches 3 sanctions programs: OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, DPRK. Hard veto — do not transact under any circumstances.
No structured label profile returned; risk established via sanctions and web sentiment instead.
Wallet is active with multiple recent inbound token transfers on Ethereum and BNB Chain, indicating continued use of a sanctioned address.
Multiple public posts identify this wallet as a Lazarus Group / Ronin Bridge exploiter wallet (OFAC sanctioned), recommending block.
Contract analysis unresolved; not material since address behaves as an EOA and sanctions veto applies.
